svg.title: Trust Architecture Reference Model
svg.desc: The Trust Architecture Reference Model. Three layers — Operational (Decision Supply Chain), Governance (Six ARAF Dimensions), and Institutional (GBI, Certification Tiers, Institutional Reliance) — connected by upward arrows representing governance posture and assessment output. Evidence Infrastructure runs as a vertical gold spine on the right. The governing question throughout: Who was responsible? What did they know? What did they do? From Trust Architecture: Making Autonomous Systems Bankable by Carly Martin, ARAF v3.0, 2026.
svg.text: Higher scores indicate greater governance risk.
Who was responsible? What did they know? What did they do?
Evidence must be present before institutional reliance is appropriate
Operational autonomy · commitment authority · oversight adequacy
Customer + vendor agreements · AI liability provisions
D1 – D6 → GBI COMPOSITE SCORE (1.0 – 5.0) + MULTIPLIER LOGIC
Six dimensions + compounding multipliers → governance posture signal
The Trust Architecture Reference Model illustrates how autonomous decision infrastructure, governance assessment,
evidence generation, and certification interact to produce institutional governance signals.
Martin, C. Trust Architecture: Making Autonomous Systems Bankable · ARAF v3.0, 2026 · trust-architecture.com
